sections.heading: Deep Dive: REST API Design
sections.body: Let's go deeper into REST API Design. This section covers the internals and advanced concepts that separate beginners from proficient developers.

code.explanation: This example shows the basic structure. In practice, you'll use REST API Design throughout your projects.

sections.heading: Practical Implementation
sections.body: Here's how to implement REST API Design in a real project. The key is to start simple and add complexity as you gain confidence.

code.explanation: This pattern shows how to structure your implementation. Adapt it to your specific use case.

sections.heading: Common Patterns & Best Practices
sections.body:
Here are the best practices for REST API Design:

1. Start Simple: Begin with the simplest implementation that works, then add complexity as needed.

2. Follow Conventions: Stick to established patterns and conventions in your codebase. Consistency makes code maintainable.

3. Test Your Work: Always test your implementation. Write tests that cover both happy paths and edge cases.

4. Document Decisions: Add comments that explain WHY, not WHAT. Future you (and teammates) will thank you.

5. Review and Refactor: Code reviews and regular refactoring keep code quality high.

sections.heading: Practice Task
sections.body: Apply what you've learned about REST API Design. Try building a small project that uses these concepts. Start with the basics and gradually add features.

summary: REST API Design is an essential concept that you'll use throughout your development journey. RESTful conventions, resource naming, versioning, and best practices. Practice the examples in this lesson and apply them to your own projects.
